question_title: Will NASA's Artemis II mission launch before May 1, 2026?
summary: Artemis II launched on April 1, 2026 at 6:35 PM EDT from Kennedy Space Center in Florida, well before the May 1, 2026 deadline. This is confirmed by NASA's official mission pages (the exact resolution sources named in the question criteria), Space.com, CBS News, and Wikipedia. The mission has progressed beyond launch — astronauts have already performed the translunar injection burn and are en route to the Moon. The resolution criteria (liftoff and clearing the launch tower before May 1, 2026) are definitively satisfied.

factors.description: Artemis II launched on April 1, 2026 at 6:35 PM EDT from Kennedy Space Center, confirmed by NASA, Space.com, CBS News, and Wikipedia
NASA official sources confirm the rocket cleared the launch tower and entered Earth orbit, then performed translunar injection burn — satisfying 'clears the launch tower' criterion
Launch date (April 1, 2026) is well before the May 1, 2026 resolution deadline

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.99 (YES)**

Artemis II has already launched. On April 1, 2026 at 6:35 PM EDT, NASA's SLS rocket carrying the Orion spacecraft with astronauts Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen lifted off from Kennedy Space Center. This is confirmed by the exact resolution sources named in the question (NASA's mission pages) as well as Space.com, CBS News, and Wikipedia. The mission has progressed well beyond launch — the crew has already performed the translunar injection burn and is on their way to the Moon. The resolution criteria are definitively satisfied.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will NASA's Artemis II mission launch before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43047)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if, before May 1, 2026, Artemis II lifts off and clears the launch tower. This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is NASA's Artemis II mission coverage page at <https://www.nasa.gov/missions/artemis/artemis-2/nasa-sets-coverage-for-artemis-ii-moon-mission/> where NASA posts launch timing and mission coverage details, together with the Artemis II mission page at <https://www.nasa.gov/artemis-ii/> if NASA records the launch there first. (NASA)
fine_print: A countdown test, tanking operation, wet dress rehearsal, scrub, or rollback does not count. Use NASA's official mission status and timing. If NASA records liftoff on April 30, 2026 local launch date, this question resolves to yes. Candidate Source: NASA's news release page for Artemis II at <https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-sets-coverage-for-first-artemis-crewed-mission-around-moon/> may be used if it posts the confirmed launch first. If the primary source is unavailable or there is a dispute, resolve using credible sources per <https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions>
